location.office_time_required: hybrid — some time in office, some from home (specific ratio not stated)

level_signals: 5 to 8 yrs of experience leading software projects
Salary range $171,600–$229,300 consistent with senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bold Generalist - High level of comfort with ambiguity, Ability to step outside their comfort zone and wear a multitude of hats as they help throughout the organization.
Detail-oriented: You care about depth of knowledge and dive deep into the inner workings of every technology domain to identify dependencies and catch risks early.
Program delivery experience:You get things done! You have shown agency by leading software projects in fast-paced or early-stage environments with over 5 to 8 yrs of experience.

Technical fluency and strong analytical, quantitative skills - Proven ability to use data and metrics to back up assumptions and recommendations. Comfortable working with engineers and understanding systems, even if you don't write code.
Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal with emphasis on ability to collaborate, influence and align efforts of disparate groups to achieve a common goal.
Strong project management: Skilled at planning, prioritising, managing risks, and adapting as things change. Proficiency in productivity, BI tools such as: Looker, Agentic AI, JIRA, Confluence etc
Growth mindset: Always open to feedback and looking to improve.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Ideal if experience is in AV or AI/ML or Automotive domains.

scope.surface: Cross-functional portfolio planning across all product categories (ADAS features, Robotaxi) and customer programs (e.g., Nissan)
scope.business_model: B2B — AI software and foundation models for automakers enabling automated driving
core_responsibilities: Develop, manage, and execute key portfolio projects, initiatives, and business opportunities
Help define the value proposition for portfolio projects, maintain detailed product roadmaps, and set milestones based on long-term vision
Conduct in-depth studies and analyses to understand the implications of proposed product changes across all business functions
Collaborate cross-functionally to establish and maintain a single source of truth for all product plans, roadmaps, data and assumptions
Own the risk register for the company and proactively build risk mitigation plans
Communicate with, influence, and manage the expectations of senior leadership
Focus on short-to-mid-term tactical plans, manage the full product lifecycle, and coordinate aspects like financials, logistics, inventory levels, and pricing

green_flags: Equity mentioned as part of package
Hybrid policy explicitly stated — not fully on-site
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every requirement
Clear scope: portfolio-level planning, not feature-owner execution
Company at growth stage with recognizable OEM customer (Nissan) — validates commercial traction
red_flags: Role is Sunnyvale-based hybrid; no fully remote option — eliminates non-Bay Area candidates
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in the JD body
Years of experience stated as '5 to 8 yrs' — unusual framing; could imply a ceiling on seniority or the range is soft
ATS URL points to a third-party (firststage.co) rather than greenhouse directly — may complicate application tracking

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years of experience)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
location.work_auth (not stated in JD body; left as unclear)
location.visa_sponsorship (not stated in JD body; left as unclear)
ambiguities: Years of experience phrasing '5 to 8 yrs' is unusual — could mean minimum 5 or a target band of 5–8; treated as min=5, max=8.
The JD references E-Verify for US candidates only, which may imply no sponsorship, but this is not explicitly stated; left as unclear.
Office time ratio for hybrid not specified beyond 'combines time together in our offices... and time spent working from home.'
